techs.name: Agriculture
techs.description: Turns dirt into policy and policy into bread.

techs.name: Toolmaking
techs.description: Civilization begins the moment someone sharpens the boring stick.

techs.name: Tribal Warfare
techs.description: Arguments, but with drums and better posture.

techs.name: Trade
techs.description: Why raid it when you can invoice it?

techs.name: Masonry
techs.description: Because 'please do not invade' works better in stone.

techs.name: Leatherworking
techs.description: Turns hides into straps, and straps into military confidence.

techs.name: Mining
techs.description: Sooner or later, empire becomes a hole in the ground with a payroll.

techs.name: Cartography
techs.description: A map is just ambition with cleaner lines.

techs.name: Irrigation
techs.description: Convincing water to report for work on time.

techs.name: Pottery
techs.description: The ancient art of storing things instead of losing them.

techs.name: Animal Husbandry
techs.description: A bold experiment in outsourcing labor to creatures with opinions.

techs.name: Bronze Working
techs.description: When sharper metal starts winning philosophical debates.

techs.name: Fortified Walls
techs.description: A polite reminder that entry is now a managed privilege.

techs.name: Road Building
techs.description: Mud loses most debates once roads arrive.

techs.name: Ledger Keeping
techs.description: Gold behaves much better once someone starts counting it.

techs.name: Maritime Trade
techs.description: A dock becomes a ledger the moment ships start keeping score.

techs.name: Signal Fires
techs.description: Smoke, but make it strategic.

techs.name: Granaries
techs.description: Because winter always arrives with excellent timing and poor manners.

techs.name: Crop Rotation
techs.description: Even fields perform better when they are not doing the same thing forever.

techs.name: Mercenary Contracts
techs.description: Loyalty is expensive, but so is losing.

techs.name: Iron Working
techs.description: The moment metal stops being useful and starts being persuasive.

techs.name: Fortification Doctrine
techs.description: A full philosophy built around not dying on your own land.

techs.name: Siegecraft
techs.description: For when knocking politely has clearly failed.

techs.name: Merchant Guilds
techs.description: At last, the traders unionize and the treasury smiles.

techs.name: Surveying
techs.description: Nothing says empire like measuring land you do not fully own yet.

techs.name: Watermills
techs.description: The river finally joins the payroll.

techs.name: Civil Survey
techs.description: Now expansion arrives with paperwork and suspicious accuracy.

techs.name: Standing Army
techs.description: A permanent answer to temporary neighbors.

techs.name: Heavy Masonry
techs.description: When your walls begin to take their job personally.

techs.name: Relay Roads
techs.description: The empire starts moving like it has somewhere to be.

techs.name: Navigation
techs.description: At last, the sea becomes a route instead of a rude suggestion.

techs.name: Beacon Towers
techs.description: At last, a skyline that warns people properly.

techs.name: Coinage
techs.description: Standardized money: fewer arguments, better greed.

techs.name: Port Infrastructure
techs.description: Harbors stop being shorelines and start becoming systems.

techs.name: Chainmail
techs.description: Thousands of tiny rings agreeing to keep organs inside.

techs.name: Engineering
techs.description: At this point, the empire runs on levers, pulleys, and audacity.

techs.name: Banking
techs.description: Money now has a house, rules, and very firm opinions.

techs.name: State Granaries
techs.description: Hunger meets bureaucracy and somehow loses.

techs.name: Steelworking
techs.description: Bronze was a phase. This is commitment.

techs.name: Logistics
techs.description: The glamorous science of making sure the army arrives with lunch.

techs.name: Cryptography
techs.description: Secrets, but organized enough to invoice.

techs.name: Civil Service
techs.description: The empire discovers forms, stamps, and sustainable annoyance.

techs.name: Arms Guilds
techs.description: Mass production, now with sharper consequences.

techs.name: Global Trade Networks
techs.description: Trade stops following roads and starts dictating them.

techs.name: Terrain Engineering
techs.description: When the mountain becomes a policy question instead of a natural fact.

techs.name: Bureaucracy
techs.description: The empire achieves its final form: forms.

techs.name: Steel
techs.description: Harder, louder, and increasingly difficult to argue with.

techs.name: Supply Trains
techs.description: War, now with timetables.

techs.name: Central Banking
techs.description: When the treasury stops being a room and becomes a worldview.

techs.name: Frontier Administration
techs.description: Expansion, but with clipboards and alarming efficiency.

techs.name: Military Academies
techs.description: At last, warfare becomes a curriculum.

techs.name: Grand Cartography
techs.description: The world is still dangerous, but now it is labeled.

techs.name: Trade Empire
techs.description: At a certain point, the empire stops funding trade and trade starts funding the empire.

techs.name: Urban Markets
techs.description: A city reaches maturity when every problem gains a price tag.
